channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: NSW's strategy for the NRL Origin decider: Focus on weather advantage and limiting key players
item.description: The article discusses Joey and Danika's analysis of the upcoming NRL Origin decider, focusing on NSW's strategic approach. With expected wet weather, the Blues' plan leverages the conditions to their ...

item.title: Kenya Invests Sh1 Trillion in Digital Infrastructure Expansion
item.description: The Kenyan government has announced a major investment of Sh1 trillion aimed at expanding telecommunications infrastructure and strengthening the digital economy. This initiative focuses on improving ...

item.title: Africa's Climate Resilience Lessons for a Super El Niño
item.description: A super El Niño, characterized by unusually warm Pacific Ocean temperatures, is expected to cause severe weather impacts globally. The article highlights Africa's experience managing climate volatilit...

item.title: Nigel Farage Faces Leadership Challenges and Financial Scrutiny in Reform UK
item.description: The article details the growing internal turmoil within Reform UK, led by Nigel Farage, as he faces mounting pressure from financial scandals, factional disputes, and strategic missteps. Key issues in...

item.title: Mariners dominate Blue Jays in 11-0 shutout as Gilbert and Arozarena shine
item.description: The Seattle Mariners delivered a commanding 11-0 victory over the Toronto Blue Jays on Saturday, showcasing their offensive prowess and pitching depth. Logan Gilbert (7-5) dominated the game, surrende...

item.title: Impeachment Trial of Sara Duterte May Be Shortened if Evidence Sufficient
item.description: House prosecution panel adviser Robert Ace Barbers stated that the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte could be shortened if the evidence presented is deemed sufficient. He emphasized tha...

item.title: Mumbai Family Still Haunted by Tree Branch Accident After 8 Years
item.description: Prakash Nayak, 43, and his family in Mumbai still live in fear of falling tree branches eight years after a fatal incident that claimed his 91-year-old aunt Leela Sukhi's life. The accident occurred o...

item.title: Corporate Security and National Security Intersections in Tech Regulation
item.description: The article explores the complex relationship between corporate security, national security, and government regulation of technology companies. It highlights debates over whether private entities shou...

item.title: Arusha Hosts International Art and Tourism Festival to Boost Creative Industry
item.description: Arusha is set to host a major international art and tourism festival in October 2026, bringing together over 1,500 artists and traders from around the world. The event, organized under the Tanzania In...

item.title: Tanzanian Businesses Encouraged to Tap into China's Trade Opportunities
item.description: The Tanzanian government is actively promoting local businesses to capitalize on expanding trade opportunities with China, a key economic partner. Deputy Minister Dennis Londo highlighted this during ...

item.title: Sandile Ndlovu Recognized for Capturing Resilience and Hope Through Photography
item.description: Photographer Sandile Ndlovu has been honored as the Standard Bank Sikuvile Journalist of the Year 2026 for his impactful photo essays that highlight resilience, hope, and the lives of ordinary South A...

item.title: France Advances to Quarter-Finals After 1-0 Win Over Paraguay
item.description: France secured a hard-fought 1-0 victory over Paraguay in a World Cup match played under extreme heat conditions, advancing to the quarter-finals for the fourth consecutive time. Kylian Mbappé, the Fr...

item.title: England Faces Altitude Challenges in World Cup 2026 Match Against Mexico
item.description: England's squad for the 2026 World Cup will have the option to use sildenafil (the blue pill) when facing Mexico in the Round of 16, according to a report by TUKO.co.ke. The decision comes as the team...

item.title: ReelsTalk discusses favoritism allegations against Lord
item.description: The article features a ReelsTalk segment questioning whether Lord, a prominent figure in religious discussions, has shown favoritism. The piece begins with a provocative headline that sparks curiosity...

item.title: Regina’s Poker Run fundraiser supports Cosmopolitan Learning Centre

item.title: France Advances to Quarter-Finals in World Cup 2026 After Supercomputer Predictions
item.description: The article discusses the 2026 FIFA World Cup qualifiers, highlighting France and Morocco as the first teams to secure quarter-final spots. France's 1-0 victory over Paraguay, secured by Kylian Mbappé...

item.title: DILG Seeks One-Year Extension for Retiring Calabarzon Police Chief
item.description: The Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) has requested a one-year extension for Police Brigadier General Hansel Marantan, the head of the Calabarzon regional police. Marantan, who is...

item.title: Country Cob Bakery Wins 2026 Australian Best Pie Competition with Malaysian Prawn Curry Pie
item.description: Ryan Khun and his brother Chan Khun of Country Cob Bakery in Springvale, Victoria, have claimed the prestigious 2026 Australian Best Pie Competition title with their innovative Malaysian prawn curry p...

item.title: Tanzania reimposes ban on political rallies amid post-election tensions
item.description: Tanzania has reimposed a ban on political rallies, marking the second time in a decade the government has restricted mass gatherings. The move comes after a violent post-election crackdown in 2025, wi...

item.title: Nairobi Joint Robbery: Two Armed Thieves Kidnap Patrons During Dinner
item.description: Two armed robbers targeted a popular joint in Nairobi's General Mathenge Road on July 4, 2026, during peak dinner hours. CCTV footage captured the incident as the suspects arrived on a motorbike, bran...
